title: Helmut Marko 'closed the door' on Yuki Tsunoda joining Haas for the 2026 F1 season
content:
Haas were ‘very interested’ in signing Yuki Tsunoda before Helmut Marko blocked the move, according to a journalist. Tsunoda lost his place on the F1 grid for 2026 after five seasons.
It seemed as if
Tsunoda’s long-awaited promotion to Red Bull
in early 2025, at the expense of Liam Lawson, would transform his career. But the Japanese driver couldn’t build on the promise he had shown at Racing Bulls.
All eyes on Yuki - Red Bull stars take over Tokyo's streets ahead of Tsunoda's team debut at this weekend's Japanese Grand Prix
With Isack Hadjar promoted and Arvid Lindblad parachuted from Formula 2 to the sister team,
Tsunoda
found himself frozen out. He remains involved with the team as a reserve driver but will likely have to leave entirely if he wants another full-time seat.
Yuki Tsunoda says he’s ‘not finished yet’ after Red Bull exit. Which team do you think he will race for next in Formula 1?
dubsgti
Featured comment
Only if Fernando decides to retire after '26. As long as Lance's daddy owns the team, Stroll will always have that seat.
dubsgti
Featured comment
Yep. If Colopinto can't do better in '26, drop him, sign Tsunoda. It's already established Yuki & Pierre could be good together.
1
dubsgti
Featured comment
I don't see that happening. Both Ocon & Bearman had strong 2025 seasons. If the keep improving in '26, Hass would probably keep them. Alpine however... It would make sense to drop Colopinto for Tsunoda.
Helmut Marko ‘snuffed out’ Haas’ interest in Yuki Tsunoda
Speaking on
The Race F1
podcast, journalist Scott Mitchell-Malm predicted that Haas will give Tsunoda a second chance in 2027. Team principal Ayao Komatsu was keen on signing him a year ago.
But despite Tsunoda’s indifferent start at
Red Bull
, Marko wanted to keep all options on the table for 2026. The irony, of course, is that the Austrian left the fold himself at the end of the year.
Tsunoda is tied to Honda
, while Haas are linked to Toyota through a title sponsorship and technical partnership. The rivalry between the Japanese manufacturers isn’t seen as an obstacle to a potential deal.
“I’m going to stick my neck out and say Yuki Tsunoda,” said Mitchell-Malm. “I think Haas is his route back into F1. Komatsu was very interested in Tsunoda 12 months ago. It couldn’t continue because Marko closed the door to keep maximum flexibility on what Red Bull could do with its drivers.
“It snuffed out that interest quite early, but in that first third of last year, Tsunoda was a bit of an interesting wildcard in that part of the driver market.
“While he’s obviously been a Honda driver up until now, I think it’s possible that, with that Toyota link, Toyota seize the opportunity for Tsunoda to become the reference for what they want to achieve with that Haas partnership, which is to be a pathway for young Japanese drivers.
“I think Tsunoda to Haas has quite a lot of sense to it once you look into it.”
Yuki Tsunoda could replace Esteban Ocon at Haas
While
Oliver Bearman is part of the Ferrari family
, he is unlikely to be promoted in 2027.
Lewis Hamilton’s contract runs for three years
and there’s no indication he will leave early.
Instead,
Tsunoda could replace Esteban Ocon
after Komatsu publicly expressed frustration with the Frenchman’s performances.
Ocon scored his first points of 2026 at the Japanese GP last time out, but he has only finished in the top 10 in five of the last 16 races. Bearman is becoming the lead driver in the team despite his inexperience.
Honda are currently supplying Aston Martin’s engines, but Tsunoda is unlikely to get a seat there because
Fernando Alonso is inclined to sign a new contract
and Lance Stroll is safe. A move to Haas looks far more realistic.

title: Karun Chandhok reacts after government official claims Indian Grand Prix will be back in 2027
content:
India’s Sports Minister has claimed that Formula 1 will return to the country by the 2027 season.
The sport is looking to add new locations to the calendar in the next few seasons, without extending it beyond the existing 24-race format.
Tracks such as Spa will be on rotational contracts
from this year, with the Belgian GP set to drop off the schedule in 2028 and 2030.
Red Bull driver Verstappen wins Austrian Grand Prix
The Portuguese Grand Prix will return from 2027
onwards at Portimao, while there was talk that the
Turkish Grand Prix would also return next year
. Now, it is being claimed that the Indian Grand Prix will be back on the calendar.
Which circuit would you like to see F1 add to the calendar?
Photos by MANAN VATSYAYANA/AFP / Dan Istitene – Formula 1/Formula 1 / Hoch Zwei/Corbis via Getty Images
donald.morgan5922
Featured comment
Kayakami
czechfog1836
Featured comment
Sepang or Korea.
david.comerford
Featured comment
Bring back Turkey!
1
India has not hosted an F1 race since 2013. It ran events at the Buddh International Circuit from 2011 to 2013, with Sebastian Vettel winning all three races.
The Indian Grand Prix would lose its spot on the calendar due to rising taxes and ticket prices to host the event, as the Uttar Pradesh government classed F1 as entertainment, not a sport.
Photo by Vince Mignott/MB Media/Getty Images
Karun Chandhok says it’s ‘too soon’ for the Indian Grand Prix in 2027
Journalist Kunal Shah posted on
X (formerly Twitter)
that India were planning to bring the Buddh International Circuit back to the calendar.
Sports Minister Mansukh Mandaviya is absolutely convinced that the race will go ahead. He said, as quoted by Shah: “There will be an F1 race in India in 2027. This is 100 per cent happening. First race will be at Buddha International Circuit.”
Former Indian F1 driver and current pundit,
Karun Chandhok
, reacted to the news via his
X account
. He says, while he applauded the ambition, that it is ‘too soon’ for the country to return by next year.
“Good to see the ambition, but 2027 is too soon for the level of work needed to make this happen,” he wrote. “Would love to see the Indian GP back but the owners need to carry out work on the track, work on the financial model & the regulatory & taxation issues. 2029 – 2030 at the earliest
Photo by Clive Mason/Getty Images
F1 say it is ‘highly unlikely’ that the Indian Grand Prix will return in 2027
F1 have since responded to the strong claim, stating that it is ‘highly unlikely’ that the Indian Grand Prix will return by 2027.
However, as quoted by Shah, the sport have said that it is a ‘market of interest’: “2027 is highly unlikely but India is a market of interest. We have many amazing fans in the country but we are not close to an agreement.”
Mandaviya added that India must plan to host a MotoGP race before it can start thinking about F1: “Given the global situation due to the ongoing Iran war, India is being seen as a safe and viable venue for sporting events, including F1.
“We plan to have a MotoGP event before F1 returns. The Federation of Motor Sports Clubs of India (FMSCI) is in talks with Formula One right now, we will be facilitators.”
